title: refactor(enter-account): dedupe stripe-webhook analytics emit handlers
author: voodoohop
pr_body_excerpt:
- Extract `emitCheckoutSessionAnalytics` and `emitPaymentIntentAnalytics` for the near-identical Stripe-webhook analytics handlers
- Add a contract-lock test asserting the paid `checkout.session.completed` Tinybird payload (previously unasserted)

Verified non-breaking: biome clean, affected tests green vs the main baseline. Net +34 lines (net) — adds a regression test; source shrinks.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

gist.headline: Tidy webhooks
gist.blurb: Cleaned up our Stripe webhook analytics handlers to keep the codebase neat and added new tests to ensure payment tracking stays perfectly reliable.
gist.summary: Refactored the Stripe webhook handlers in enter.pollinations.ai by extracting emitCheckoutSessionAnalytics and emitPaymentIntentAnalytics to deduplicate code, and added a contract-lock test for Tinybird payloads.
gist.impact: The codebase is now cleaner and more maintainable, with guaranteed reliability for checkout.session.completed analytics tracking.
gist.visual_concept: The bee mascot organizing files or cleaning up a messy room of receipts (representing Stripe webhooks/payments). The Nomnom creature eating duplicate code snippets.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing a tiny maid headband, sweeping up duplicate code snippets while the round tan Nomnom creature happily eats them. A retro terminal in the background displays a green checkmark. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Chunky pixels, warm lighting, lo-fi aesthetic like Stardew Valley.
